lessons.title: File Handling
lessons.description: Read and write text files safely.

questions.questionText: Which mode opens a file for writing?
Which method reads all file contents as one string?
Arrange to append one line to a file.
questions.explanation: Open in append mode then write.
a writes at end without truncating.

questions.codeSnippet:
with open('notes.txt', w) as f:
    f.write('ok')
questions.buggyCode:
with open('notes.txt', w) as f:
    f.write('ok')
questions.bugDescription: File mode must be a string literal.
questions.explanation: open expects the mode as text such as 'w' or 'r'.

lessons.title: List Comprehensions
lessons.codeExample: squares = [x * x for x in range(5)]

questions.questionText: What does [x*2 for x in [1,2]] produce?

questions.codeSnippet: evens = [x for x ___ range(6) if x % 2 == 0]
questions.explanation: Comprehensions use for x in iterable.

questions.questionText: Where does condition go in list comprehension?
questions.explanation: Expression for x in iterable if condition.
Create source list, derive new list, print.
They build lists from iterables compactly.

slides.title: Welcome to List Comprehensions
